ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Oct-02-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kevin O'Brien for Melikaya White (Administrator).
Thomas Cain for Nancy Jo Dinkens and 15 intestate Heirs.
Susan Devermont for American Research Bureau ("ARB").
Susan Aglietti Guardian Ad Litem for the Minors (4).

The Court stated that there are no time sheets for the Guardian Ad Litem, that it is assuming that all of the Parties understand that no funds will be paid out of the reserve without a Court order, and that it needs to know what is going to the Guardian Ad Litem, how she earned it and that it needs to be supported. The Court also questions how it can make a determination as to what ARB did to earn the fees and stated that it has no idea how much time was put into this. Ms. Algietti stated that she can submit a declaration and time sheets. Mr. O'Brien stated that the Guardian Ad Litem will take the distributions and set up blocked accounts for the Minors. Ms. Devermont stated that it has always been ARB's business practice to utilize contingency fee agreements and it is common in the industry, that it is almost impossible to provide approximation of time, that there was a trial, an appeal and a tremendous amount of work done by the Attorney, that they don't calculate and resource on a case by case basis, and that all of the Heirs have not complained. Ms. Devermont also stated that if it had not been for ARB, the wrong Heirs would have received distribution, that the Heirs are receiving over $1,000,000.00, made reference to the Estate of Wright, stated that ARB has a 35% contingency fee, that the Court of Appeal has consistently upheld that, spoke of the level of risk to ARB, and stated that a lot of work was done and that the business model of this company is not to keep time sheets. In addition, Ms. Devermont stated that other Courts all over California have been fine with the contingency fee agreement, that she doesn't understand why this Court has taken this position and that they have another case here that is on appeal for the same reason. Mr. Cain stated that he represents People that have waited for five years, that they have been through two years of appeal, that they are desperate to receive this money and that the Court has the ability to grant this today. The Court spoke of its obligation and duty to the Heirs to oversee and determine reasonableness of fees. Ms. Devermont stated that she submitted a declaration that explains how the business is framed, that costs are not the bulk of these fees and that it is the tremendous amount of work in locating the Heirs. Mr. Cain stated that he does not think there can be a calculation for the risk involved. Ms. Algietti raised the issue of one of the Minors turning 18 on October 8th. Ms. Devermont stated that with respect to the four Minors, ARB is waiving the agreement and that she believes the order requires a re-calculation. The Petition for Approval of First Account and Report, etc. is under submission. Ms. Algietti provided orders (4) for the blocked accounts.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Reporter: Raquel Sharp, CSR #10619, 925-384-0003, raquel@transcriptlegal.com

Petition To Determine Heirship
Off Calendar Nov-19-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Thomas Cain for Nancy Jo Dinkens (Petitioner).
Kevin O'Brien for Melikaya White (Administrator/Respondent).
Susan Aglietti Guardian Ad Litem for the Minors of the late Johnny Elisher.

Ms. Aglietti stated that she was made aware that the amounts the Minors are to receive are relatively small amounts, that she wants to find a way to reduce any further expenses, that she could set up time deposits in blocked accounts to mature when each one of the Minors reaches the age of majority and suggest the accounts be held in her name since she is the Guardian Ad Litem. Ms. Aglietti also stated that she believes some of the Children have a Guardian of the person, but she is not sure, that she believes monies (larger amounts) from other settlements were placed in some sort of insurance, and that they are all out of State and she has no control. The Court questions whether there should be a Guardian of the Estate, stated that the Guardian Ad Litem should be able to get information and then distribute the Estate, and suggests Ms. Aglietti communicate with the Parents, Guardians and Attorneys. The Court also stated that it would like a revised order setting forth how they propose distributing the money and whether there is a guardianship of the estate, and directed Ms. Aglietti to speak with the bank to see if they will open an account here in the name of the guardianship of the estate. Mr. Cain asked if both petitions could be approved subject to filing of a report of the Guardian Ad Litem, stated that three Minors have guardianships, that one doesn't, that there has been no administrator of the estate for three years and that the parties were told they have to dismiss the appeal. Mr. O'Brien stated that once the settlement is approved, his Client will then dismiss the appeal. The Petition to Determine Heirship, and the Petition for Court Confirmation of Settlement Agreement are under submission. The Petition for Authority to Participate in Proceedings to Determine Heirship is moot. The First and Final Report of Administrator on Waiver of Account, etc. is off calendar.

Petition For Authority To Participate In Proceedings To Determine Heirship
Off Calendar Feb-22-2016 Continued To Apr-25-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Leahy for petitioners Melikaya White et al.,; Thomas Cain for objector Nancy Jo Denkins

The Court asked if the dispute was appropriate for mediation. Mr. Leahy stated that if the dispute is not resolved by motion for judgment on the pleadings, it could be resolved by a one-hour evidentiary hearing. Mr. Cain responded that he believed tha intestate heirs rights were restricted under the Probate Code, and his client would not be willing to proceed by mediation at this time. He stated that if the Court were to deny the Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings, it should proceed to grant the amended petition to determine heirship. Mr. Leahy stated he was ready to submit the matter for determination. Mr. Cain replied that he would like to submit additional briefing on the issue of standing. Following a discussion regarding a briefing schedule, the Court set a deadline of December 21, 2015 for Mr. Cain to submit supplemental briefing, and gave Mr. Leahy a deadline of January 4, 2016 for filing of a response to the supplemental brief, at which time the matter will be deemed submitted.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not reported).
